---
title: "Python Companion"
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Python Companion}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r}
#| include: false
#| eval: false
library(freestiler)
```

## Overview

freestiler has a Python companion package that shares the same Rust engine. It
accepts GeoDataFrames (via GeoPandas) and produces the same PMTiles output. The
Python package lives in the `python/` subdirectory of the freestiler repository.

## Installation

Install from source using pip (requires Rust toolchain):

```bash
cd python
pip install -e .
```

Or build with maturin directly:

```bash
cd python
maturin develop --release
```

## Basic usage

```python
import geopandas as gpd
from freestiler import freestile

gdf = gpd.read_file("nc.shp")

freestile(gdf, "nc_counties.pmtiles", layer_name="counties")
```

```
Creating MLT tiles (zoom 0-14) for 100 features across 1 layer...
  Tiling layer 'counties' (zoom 0-14)...
Created nc_counties.pmtiles (65.2 KB)
```

## R vs Python API

The Python API mirrors R closely, with Pythonic conventions:

| Feature | R | Python |
|---------|---|--------|
| Input type | sf data frame | GeoDataFrame |
| Multi-layer | Named list of sf objects | Dict of GeoDataFrames |
| Default format | `"mlt"` | `"mlt"` |
| Zoom range | `min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 14` | `min_zoom=0, max_zoom=14` |
| Feature dropping | `drop_rate = 2.5` | `drop_rate=2.5` |
| Base zoom | `base_zoom = 10` | `base_zoom=10` |
| Point clustering | `cluster_distance = 50` | `cluster_distance=50` |
| Feature coalescing | `coalesce = TRUE` | `coalesce=True` |
| Layer helper | `freestile_layer()` | (use dict directly) |

## MVT output

```python
freestile(gdf, "nc_mvt.pmtiles",
    layer_name="counties",
    tile_format="mvt"
)
```

## Multi-layer tiles

Pass a dictionary mapping layer names to GeoDataFrames:

```python
import geopandas as gpd
from freestiler import freestile

counties = gpd.read_file("counties.shp")
roads = gpd.read_file("roads.shp")

freestile(
    {"counties": counties, "roads": roads},
    "layers.pmtiles"
)
```

## Feature dropping and base_zoom

```python
freestile(gdf, "dropped.pmtiles",
    layer_name="counties",
    drop_rate=2.5,
    base_zoom=10
)
```

## Point clustering

```python
points = gpd.read_file("points.shp")

freestile(points, "clustered.pmtiles",
    layer_name="points",
    cluster_distance=50,
    cluster_maxzoom=8
)
```

## Feature coalescing

```python
freestile(gdf, "coalesced.pmtiles",
    layer_name="counties",
    coalesce=True
)
```

## Viewing tiles

### With maplibre-py

```python
import maplibre
from maplibre import Map, MapOptions
from maplibre.sources import VectorTileSource
from maplibre.layers import Layer, LayerType

source = VectorTileSource(url="pmtiles://nc_counties.pmtiles")

layer = Layer(
    type=LayerType.FILL,
    id="counties",
    source="counties-source",
    source_layer="counties",
    paint={"fill-color": "#00897b", "fill-opacity": 0.5}
)

m = Map(MapOptions(center=[-79.5, 35.5], zoom=6))
m.add_source("counties-source", source)
m.add_layer(layer)
m
```

### In a web browser

PMTiles files can be served from any static file host and viewed with MapLibre
GL JS using the [PMTiles protocol](https://docs.protomaps.com/pmtiles/):

```html
<script src="https://unpkg.com/pmtiles/dist/pmtiles.js"></script>
<script src="https://unpkg.com/maplibre-gl/dist/maplibre-gl.js"></script>
<script>
  let protocol = new pmtiles.Protocol();
  maplibregl.addProtocol("pmtiles", protocol.tile);

  new maplibregl.Map({
    container: "map",
    style: {
      version: 8,
      sources: {
        counties: {
          type: "vector",
          url: "pmtiles://nc_counties.pmtiles"
        }
      },
      layers: [{
        id: "county-fill",
        type: "fill",
        source: "counties",
        "source-layer": "counties",
        paint: { "fill-color": "#00897b", "fill-opacity": 0.5 }
      }]
    }
  });
</script>
```
